# CSS / Tailwind Audit â€” `web/next/src`

Date: 2026-06-28
Scope: all `*.tsx` under `web/next/src`
Mode: READ-ONLY. No code, page, or config was changed. This is an inventory + proposal only.

## How to read this

Three surfaces are tracked separately:

1. **App/dashboard** â€” `app/(protected)`, `app/(console)`, `components/sidebar`, `components/dashboard`
2. **Marketing/public** â€” `app/page.tsx`, `app/hire`, `app/resume`, `app/waitlist`, `components/navbar`, `components/blog`
3. **UI primitives** â€” `components/ui` (shadcn baseline)

> NOTE â€” `components/ui` values are **shadcn defaults**. They are the source scale, not drift. They are _generated_: the sync wipes `components/ui/`, so any change must go through the post-sync `shadcn-customize.ts` script, never a direct edit. They are reported here only as the baseline that app + marketing code should align to.

Headline finding: the design system is **already remarkably consistent**. Spacing follows the Tailwind ladder, `text-sm` / `size-4` / `rounded-lg` dominate cleanly, and almost every arbitrary-value class is a shadcn `data-[...]`/`group-data-[...]` _variant selector_ (not a dimension). The genuine drift is small and concentrated in: (a) the marketing pages (`page.tsx` is internally consistent but `hire`/`resume` run their own scale), (b) a handful of one-off pixel/rem dimensions, and (c) `api-status` width.

---

## 1. Spacing

### 1a. `gap-*` (numeric)

| count | value   | count | value   |
| ----: | ------- | ----: | ------- |
|    51 | gap-2   |     4 | gap-0   |
|    27 | gap-1.5 |     3 | gap-6   |
|    20 | gap-4   |     3 | gap-5   |
|    20 | gap-1   |     2 | gap-8   |
|     8 | gap-2.5 |     1 | gap-7.5 |
|     8 | gap-0.5 |     1 | gap-12  |
|     7 | gap-3   |       |         |

Dominant: **gap-2** (flex rows), **gap-1.5** (tight UI), **gap-4** / **gap-1**.

Drift:

- `gap-7.5` â€” **`components/navbar/home.tsx:98`** (main nav). Half-step + only use. Should be `gap-6` or `gap-8`.
- `gap-12` â€” `app/page.tsx` (1x, large feature grid; acceptable as a section-level value but it is the only one).
- `gap-x-*` / `gap-y-*` directional gaps are all 1x each (`gap-x-8/4/3/2`, `gap-y-8/1/0.5`) â€” these are localized layout one-offs, not a convention.

### 1b. `space-y-*`

| count | value                               |
| ----: | ----------------------------------- |
|    13 | space-y-8                           |
|     7 | space-y-4                           |
|     6 | space-y-1                           |
|     4 | space-y-2                           |
|     2 | space-y-16                          |
|     2 | space-y-0.5                         |
|     1 | space-y-1.5 / space-y-0 / space-x-2 |

Dominant: **space-y-8** then **space-y-4**.
Drift: `space-y-16` (2x, both on the `hire`/`resume` page wrappers â€” see cross-surface note), `space-y-1.5` (1x).

### 1c. Padding â€” `p-` / `px-` / `py-` / `p{trbl}-`

`px-*` distribution:

| count | value  |
| ----: | ------ |
|    24 | px-5   |
|    17 | px-2.5 |
|    15 | px-1.5 |
|    13 | px-2   |
|     6 | px-4   |
|     5 | px-8   |
|     4 | px-1   |
|     3 | px-3   |
|     2 | px-6   |

`px-5` is the **marketing container gutter** (all 24 in `page.tsx`/`hire`/`resume`). `px-2.5`/`px-1.5`/`px-2` are the **shadcn UI control** paddings. These two scales never collide (different surfaces) â€” that is fine, but worth knowing `px-5` is a marketing-only convention.

`py-*` distribution:

| count | value                       | note                                    |
| ----: | --------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
|    25 | py-1                        | UI controls                             |
|    13 | py-1.5                      | UI controls                             |
|     8 | py-2                        |                                         |
|     6 | py-24                       | **marketing section rhythm (page.tsx)** |
|     4 | py-0.5                      |                                         |
|     3 | py-2.5                      |                                         |
|     2 | py-36                       | **hire/resume wrapper only**            |
|     1 | py-8 / py-6 / py-16 / py-12 |                                         |

`p-*`: `p-0` (17), `p-1` (11), `p-4` (9), `p-2` (9), `p-2.5` (3), `p-6` (2), `p-5` (2), `p-8` (1), `p-1.5` (1). The `p-5` is a marketing card padding (`page.tsx` code blocks); `p-2.5`/`p-1.5` are UI-control half-steps from shadcn.

Directional one-offs of note (`p{trbl}`): `pl-7` (17x â€” sidebar menu indentation, a real convention), `pl-1.5`/`pr-1.5` (UI), `pr-8` (8x â€” alert/input affordance), then a long tail of 1x values (`pr-20`, `pr-18`, `pl-3.5`, `pb-2.5`).

### 1d. Margin â€” `m{xytrbl}-`

Dominant: **mb-2** (25x), **mb-4** (11x), **mt-1** (10x). Long tail of 1x values: `mb-48`, `mb-24`, **`mb-18`** (half-of-nothing; `floating-trigger.tsx:18`), `mt-24`, `mt-10`, `mx-3.5`, `mt-2.5`/`mr-2.5` (half-steps).
Negative margins are all small (`-mx-1` 6x, `-mt-1` 3x) â€” used for optical alignment inside shadcn, fine.

### Proposed spacing scale

Lean on what already dominates; drop half-steps outside `components/ui`:

- **Inline gap:** `gap-1` (tightest) â†’ `gap-1.5` (UI) â†’ `gap-2` (default) â†’ `gap-3` â†’ `gap-4` â†’ `gap-6` â†’ `gap-8`. Remove `gap-7.5`, `gap-2.5` (app/marketing) in favor of `gap-2`/`gap-3`.
- **Stack:** `space-y-{1,2,4,8}`; reserve `space-y-16` for full-page section rhythm only.
- **Padding (app/marketing):** `p-{0,1,2,4,6}`, `px-{4,5,8}`, `py-{2,6,12,24}`. Keep shadcn's `*-1.5`/`*-2.5` confined to `components/ui`.
- **Margin:** `m{t,b}-{1,2,4,8}`; standardize section bottoms on `mb-4`/`mb-8`/`mb-16`. Retire `mb-18`, `mt-2.5`, `mr-2.5` in app/marketing.

---

## 2. Width / Height / Size

### 2a. Icon sizing â€” `size-*` (the most-used family)

| count | value   | count | value                        |
| ----: | ------- | ----: | ---------------------------- |
|    83 | size-4  |     8 | size-3.5                     |
|    23 | size-6  |     7 | size-2                       |
|    22 | size-8  |     5 | size-7                       |
|    16 | size-5  |     4 | size-10                      |
|    15 | size-12 |     2 | size-2.5                     |
|     9 | size-3  |     1 | size-9 / size-7.5 / size-4.5 |

`size-4` is the overwhelming default icon size (shadcn standard). `size-3.5` (small-button icon) and `size-3` are shadcn's small variants.

Drift:

- `size-7.5` â€” **`components/devtools.tsx:49`**. Half-step, 1x. Use `size-7` or `size-8`.
- `size-4.5` â€” **`app/hire/page.tsx:342`** (inline X icon). Half-step, 1x. Use `size-4` or `size-5`.

Marketing uses big decorative icons (`size-6`/`size-12`) that the app never does â€” expected (feature illustrations vs. UI affordances).

### 2b. `w-*` / `h-*` (numeric)

`h-*` dominant: `h-8` (19x, control height), `h-12` (9x), `h-7` (7x), `h-5` (5x). `w-*` dominant: `w-0` (13x, collapse), `w-5`/`w-3`.

Drift:

- `w-45` â€” **`components/api-status.tsx:26,39,51`** (3x). `w-45` is a valid Tailwind v4 spacing multiple (11.25rem) but it is an off-ladder fixed width used purely to stop the status label from reflowing. Prefer `min-w-*` on the ladder (`min-w-48`) or `whitespace-nowrap`.
- `h-[18.4px]`, `h-[14px]`, `h-[calc(--spacing(5.25))]` â€” all in `components/ui` (shadcn). Baseline, not drift.

### 2c. `max-w-*`

| count | value     | count | value                 |
| ----: | --------- | ----: | --------------------- |
|    17 | max-w-3xl |     3 | max-w-md              |
|     8 | max-w-sm  |     3 | max-w-4xl             |
|     7 | max-w-6xl |     1 | max-w-xl / none / max |
|     6 | max-w-2xl |       |                       |

Two clean conventions:

- **Marketing:** content column = `max-w-3xl`, outer container = `max-w-6xl`, sub-copy = `max-w-2xl`. Internally consistent across `page.tsx`.
- **UI:** `max-w-sm`/`max-w-xs` (shadcn cards/dialogs).

Only one arbitrary: `max-w-[calc(100%-2rem)]` (dialog, `components/ui` â€” shadcn baseline).

### 2d. `min-w-*` / `min-h-*`

`min-w-0` (11x, flex-truncation idiom â€” keep). `min-w-36`/`min-w-32` (sidebar dropdowns). `min-w-[96px]` and `min-w-[calc(...)]` are in `components/ui`. `min-h-screen`/`min-h-svh`/`min-h-dvh` are mixed (see cross-surface note). `min-h-[700px]` â€” **`app/page.tsx:230`** (hero min-height, 1x arbitrary).

### Proposed width/size scale

- **Icons:** `size-3` / `size-3.5` (UI-small only) / **`size-4` (default)** / `size-5` / `size-6` / `size-8`. Eliminate `size-7.5` and `size-4.5`.
- **Control heights:** `h-7` (sm) / `h-8` (default) / `h-9` / `h-10` / `h-12` (lg).
- **Content widths:** `max-w-sm`/`max-w-md` (forms/cards) Â· `max-w-2xl` (sub-copy) Â· `max-w-3xl` (prose) Â· `max-w-6xl` (page container). This is already the de-facto set â€” codify it.
- Replace fixed `w-45` with a ladder `min-w-*` + `text-center`.

---

## 3. Radius

| count | value                 | count | value        |
| ----: | --------------------- | ----: | ------------ |
|    64 | rounded-lg            |     7 | rounded-none |
|    34 | rounded-md            |     6 | rounded-sm   |
|    24 | rounded-full          |     5 | rounded-t    |
|    24 | rounded (=DEFAULT)    |     2 | rounded-tl   |
|     9 | rounded-r / rounded-l |     1 | rounded-4xl  |
|     8 | rounded-b             |       |              |
|     7 | rounded-xl            |       |              |

`rounded-lg` is the house radius (tied to `--radius: 0.625rem` in `globals.css`). Marketing is fully `rounded-lg` (23 of 26). App uses `rounded-md` (shadcn control radius).

Arbitrary radii (all in `components/ui`, shadcn baseline): `rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),10px)]` (5x), `rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),12px)]` (3x), `rounded-[calc(var(--radius)-3px)]`, `rounded-[2px]`, `rounded-[4px]`, `rounded-[inherit]`. These derive from the `--radius-*` token ladder defined in `globals.css` (`--radius-sm/md/lg/xl/2xl/3xl/4xl`) â€” leave as-is.

Drift: `rounded-4xl` (1x) â€” verify it is intentional (it maps to `--radius + 16px`); otherwise an outlier vs the `lg` norm.

### Proposed radius scale

Token-driven, already in place: `rounded-sm` / `rounded-md` (controls) / **`rounded-lg` (default surfaces)** / `rounded-xl` / `rounded-full`. No new values needed. Keep arbitrary `min(var(--radius-md), â€¦)` confined to `components/ui`.

---

## 4. Typography

### 4a. Font size â€” `text-{xs..7xl}`

| count | value     | count | value    |
| ----: | --------- | ----: | -------- |
|   109 | text-sm   |     6 | text-4xl |
|    32 | text-xs   |     3 | text-xl  |
|    18 | text-lg   |     3 | text-2xl |
|    16 | text-base |     2 | text-6xl |
|    10 | text-3xl  |     2 | text-5xl |
|       |           |     1 | text-7xl |

`text-sm` is the universal body/UI size (81 of 109 are in `components/ui`). `text-xs` = secondary/labels. Display sizes (`3xl`â€“`7xl`) live exclusively in marketing.

Section-title pattern in `page.tsx` is **perfectly consistent**: every `<h2>` is `text-3xl font-bold tracking-tight sm:text-4xl` (7 occurrences), hero `<h1>` is `text-5xl sm:text-6xl lg:text-7xl`. Codify these two as the marketing heading tokens.

Arbitrary sub-rem sizes:

- `text-[0.6rem]` â€” **`components/sidebar/dashboard/user-actions.tsx:24`** and **`components/sidebar/docs/footer.tsx:20`** (version-label badges). 2x, off-scale (below `text-xs`/0.75rem). Either accept as an intentional micro-label token or bump to `text-xs`.
- `text-[0.8rem]` â€” all in `components/ui` (calendar, button-sm, toggle-sm). shadcn baseline.

### 4b. Font weight

| count | value         |
| ----: | ------------- |
|    56 | font-medium   |
|    14 | font-semibold |
|    11 | font-normal   |
|    11 | font-bold     |

`font-medium` dominates (shadcn label/button default). **Cross-surface split on emphasis:** marketing headings use `font-bold` (10x) + `font-semibold` (12x); the app rarely emphasizes (mostly `font-normal`, 5x). Marketing `hire`/`resume` headings use `font-semibold`; `page.tsx` headings use `font-bold`. Pick one for marketing display headings.

### 4c. Leading & tracking

`leading-*`: `snug` (4), `none` (4), `normal` (2), `tight` (1) â€” sparse, no strong convention.
`tracking-*`: `tracking-tight` (10x â€” all marketing headings, good), `tracking-wide` (9x â€” Caveat-font `hire`/`resume` headings), `tracking-widest` (4x). Two heading idioms (`tight` for page.tsx, `wide` for the handwritten resume aesthetic) coexist.

### Proposed type scale

- **Body/UI:** `text-xs` (meta) Â· **`text-sm` (default)** Â· `text-base` (marketing body) Â· `text-lg` (lead).
- **Marketing headings:** section `text-3xl font-bold tracking-tight sm:text-4xl`; hero `text-5xl font-bold tracking-tight sm:text-6xl lg:text-7xl`. (Already the de-facto pattern in `page.tsx`.)
- Resolve `font-semibold` vs `font-bold` for marketing headings (recommend `font-bold` to match `page.tsx`, the primary surface).
- Replace `text-[0.6rem]` with `text-xs`, or formalize a `--text-2xs` token if a true micro-label is wanted.

---

## 5. Responsive variants

Prefix usage across the whole scope:

| prefix | count |
| ------ | ----: |
| sm:    |    45 |
| md:    |    29 |
| lg:    |     9 |
| xl:    |     2 |
| 2xl:   |     2 |

Breakpoint discipline is good and mobile-first. Top responsive pairs: `sm:text-4xl` (6, heading scale-up), `sm:max-w-sm` (6), `sm:flex-row` (5, stackâ†’row), `sm:text-6xl`/`lg:text-7xl` (hero), `md:grid-cols-2`/`lg:grid-cols-3` (feature grids). No drift â€” these are textbook. Note `xl:`/`2xl:` are barely used (2 each), so the layout effectively tops out at `lg`.

---

## 6. Arbitrary values `-[â€¦]`

Almost all arbitrary classes in the codebase are **shadcn variant _selectors_** (`data-[side=â€¦]`, `group-data-[collapsible=icon]`, `has-[>svg]`, `aria-[orientation=â€¦]`, `transition-[â€¦]`, `data-[state=â€¦]`), not dimensional values. Those are correct usage and out of scope for "drift."

The genuine arbitrary _dimensions/colors_ outside `components/ui`:

| file:line                                          | class                                   | note                                                |
| -------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `app/page.tsx:228`                                 | `bg-[linear-gradient(...#80808012...)]` | hero grid pattern (incl. hardcoded hex, see Â§7)     |
| `app/page.tsx:228`                                 | `bg-size-[20px_20px]`                   | grid cell size                                      |
| `app/page.tsx:228`                                 | `mask-[radial-gradient(...)]`           | hero fade mask                                      |
| `app/page.tsx:230`                                 | `min-h-[700px]`                         | hero min height                                     |
| `app/waitlist/page.tsx:116`                        | `-left-[9999px]`                        | visually-hidden honeypot offset (intentional idiom) |
| `components/sidebar/dashboard/user-actions.tsx:24` | `text-[0.6rem]`                         | version label                                       |
| `components/sidebar/docs/footer.tsx:20`            | `text-[0.6rem]`                         | version label                                       |

Everything else arbitrary (`rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),â€¦)]`, `h-[18.4px]`, `w-[32px]`, `ring-[3px]`, `border-[1.5px]`, `p-[3px]`, `min-w-[96px]`, `translate-*-[â€¦]`, `shadow-[â€¦]`) lives in `components/ui` and is **shadcn baseline** â€” do not hand-edit.

---

## 7. Hardcoded colors

No raw `rgb()/rgba()/hsl()` in any `.tsx`. Hex literals are confined to:

| file:line                        | color                                                         | context                                               |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `lib/og-image.tsx:50,66,81,92`   | `#000`, `#1a1a1a`, `#666`, `#fff`, `#a0a0a0`                  | OG image (Satori `next/og`)                           |
| `app/og/home/route.tsx:12,29,40` | `#000`, `#1a1a1a`, `#fff`, `#a0a0a0`                          | OG image                                              |
| `app/page.tsx:228`               | `#80808012`                                                   | hero grid line color in a `bg-[linear-gradient(...)]` |
| `components/ui/chart.tsx:66`     | `#ccc`, `#fff`                                                | recharts selector overrides (shadcn baseline)         |
| `components/ui/button.tsx:15`    | `color-mix(in oklch, var(--secondary), var(--foreground) 5%)` | uses tokens, not a raw color                          |

The OG-image hex values are **unavoidable**: `next/og` / Satori renders with inline styles and cannot resolve Tailwind tokens or CSS variables, so literal colors are correct there. The only token candidate is the **`#80808012` hero grid line** in `page.tsx:228` â€” consider a `--color-grid` token or a `border`-token-based pattern for theme-awareness, though as a subtle decorative grid it is low priority.

---

## Cross-surface inconsistencies (the 4 that matter)

1. **Marketing page scale is split: `page.tsx` vs `hire`/`resume`.** The home page uses `py-24` section rhythm, `text-3xl sm:text-4xl font-bold tracking-tight` headings, and `max-w-6xl/3xl` containers. `hire`/`resume` use a separate scale: `py-36`, `space-y-16`, and Caveat-font `text-3xl font-semibold tracking-wide` headings. The handwritten-resume aesthetic is likely intentional, but it means "a marketing page" has no single spacing/heading token â€” confirm and document the divergence, or align the wrappers (`py-36`â†’`py-24`).

2. **Heading weight: `font-bold` (page.tsx) vs `font-semibold` (hire/resume).** Same role (top-level heading), two weights. Pick one for the primary surface.

3. **Viewport-height idiom is inconsistent:** `min-h-screen` (3x) vs `min-h-svh` (2x) vs `min-h-dvh` (1x) across full-page wrappers. `dvh`/`svh` are the modern mobile-safe choices; `screen` is the legacy one. Standardize (recommend `min-h-dvh`).

4. **`px-5` (marketing) vs `px-2/2.5/1.5` (app/UI) gutters never reconcile.** Not a bug â€” different surfaces â€” but there is no shared "container gutter" token. App pages lean on shadcn-internal padding; marketing hardcodes `px-5`. If a shared layout container is ever introduced, pick one gutter value.

---

## Top fixes (ranked by impact)

1. **Decide + document the marketing scale.** Either declare `hire`/`resume` a deliberately separate aesthetic in the docs, or align them to the `page.tsx` tokens (`py-24`, `font-bold`, `tracking-tight`). This is the single biggest source of "two ways to do the same thing." (cross-surface #1, #2)
2. **`gap-7.5` â†’ `gap-6`/`gap-8`** in `components/navbar/home.tsx:98`. Off-ladder half-step on the most-visible nav.
3. **`size-7.5` â†’ `size-7`/`size-8`** (`components/devtools.tsx:49`) and **`size-4.5` â†’ `size-4`/`size-5`** (`app/hire/page.tsx:342`). Two off-ladder icon sizes.
4. **`w-45` on `api-status.tsx` (3x) â†’ ladder `min-w-48` + `whitespace-nowrap`.** Fixed off-ladder width fighting text reflow.
5. **Standardize viewport-height to `min-h-dvh`** across full-page wrappers (replace `min-h-screen`/`min-h-svh`). (cross-surface #3)
6. **`text-[0.6rem]` (2x sidebar version labels) â†’ `text-xs`** (or formalize a `--text-2xs` token if a true micro-label is needed).
7. **`mb-18` (`floating-trigger.tsx:18`) â†’ `mb-16`/`mb-20`** ladder value.
8. **Codify the heading/container/icon tokens** that are _already_ dominant (`text-3xl sm:text-4xl` section title, `max-w-3xl/6xl`, `size-4`, `rounded-lg`, `text-sm`) into a short design-tokens doc so future code converges instead of re-deriving.
9. **Consider a `--color-grid` token** for the `#80808012` hero grid in `page.tsx:228` (theme-awareness; low priority).
10. **Leave `components/ui` alone.** Every "exotic" arbitrary value (`rounded-[min(...)]`, `h-[18.4px]`, `text-[0.8rem]`, recharts `#ccc/#fff`) is shadcn baseline; changes there must go through the post-sync `shadcn-customize.ts` script, not direct edits.

---

### Appendix â€” surface segmentation (key families)

| family      | App/dashboard                   | Marketing                                | UI (shadcn)                                     |
| ----------- | ------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| text size   | `text-sm`/`text-xs` (UI labels) | `text-sm/lg/3xl/4xl` + display           | `text-sm` (81), `text-xs` (23), `text-base` (8) |
| font weight | `font-normal` mostly            | `font-medium/semibold/bold`              | `font-medium` (39), `font-normal` (6)           |
| icon size   | `size-4` (5), `size-8`/`size-7` | `size-6`/`size-12` decorative + `size-4` | `size-4` (62) baseline                          |
| rounded     | `rounded-md`                    | `rounded-lg` (23/26)                     | full ladder incl. `min(...)` arbitrary          |
| px gutter   | `px-2`/`px-1.5`/`px-1`          | `px-5` (24)                              | `px-2.5`/`px-1.5`/`px-2`                        |
| max-w       | mixed (1x each)                 | `max-w-3xl`(17)/`6xl`/`2xl`              | `max-w-sm`(8)/`xs`                              |

---

## Forms & Dialogs

Date: 2026-06-28 Â· Scope: `web/next/src` Â· Branch: `feat/dashboard-shell` (canary-based). READ-ONLY follow-up to the token audit above. This section analyzes forms and dialogs as **structural patterns** (layout + primitive usage), not token frequency.

> SCOPE NOTE â€” the passkey forms/dialogs live on a separate parked branch and are **out of scope** here. This audit covers the three forms present on `feat/dashboard-shell`: `components/access.tsx`, `app/waitlist/page.tsx`, `components/sidebar/dashboard/org-switcher.tsx`.

### Forms

**De-facto convention (the two dialog forms agree):** `access.tsx` and `org-switcher.tsx` are nearly identical and define the house pattern:

- Validation: **`@tanstack/react-form` + zod** schema wired into `validators.onSubmit`/`onChange` (access adds `onBlur`). All three forms use this stack â€” no raw/uncontrolled form anywhere.
- Markup: native `<form>` (with `id` + `e.preventDefault(); form.handleSubmit()`) wrapping a single shadcn **`<FieldGroup>`** â†’ **`<form.Field>`** render-prop â†’ **`<Field data-invalid={isInvalid}>`** â†’ **`<FieldLabel htmlFor>`** + **`<Input>`** + conditional **`<FieldError errors={field.state.meta.errors}>`**.
- Invalid wiring: `const isInvalid = field.state.meta.isTouched && !field.state.meta.isValid`, fed to both `Field data-invalid` and `Input aria-invalid` (drives the destructive ring in `input.tsx:12`). Identical in all three.
- Submit button: **full-width** (`w-full cursor-pointer`), `variant="secondary"`, **leading spinner** (`<RiLoaderLine className="size-5 animate-spin" />`) gated on pending, **disabled-while-pending**.
- Input: bare shadcn `<Input>` (`h-8` default) with only `className="focus:placeholder:opacity-0"`.

So the Field-primitive convention IS followed in all three forms â€” none hand-rolls `<label>` or error markup. The deviations are in **spacing layering** and in waitlist's **deliberately different inline layout**.

| file:line                                                                                               | issue                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | fix                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `access.tsx:107` / `org-switcher.tsx:169`                                                               | `<form className="space-y-4">` wraps a `<FieldGroup>` (which already owns `gap-5`, `field.tsx:46`) **plus** the submit `<Button>`. The `space-y-4` only ever spaces FieldGroupâ†”Button (the single Field inside is spaced by FieldGroup's `gap-5`). Two competing rhythms (4 vs 5) for what reads as one stack.                                                                                                                                        | Drop `space-y-4`; put the Button **inside** the `<FieldGroup>` so its `gap-5` governs the whole stack (shadcn's intended usage â€” FieldGroup is the form-level stack container). Or, if Button must stay outside, standardize the wrapper gap to match (`space-y-5`).                                     |
| `access.tsx:113` / `org-switcher.tsx:175`                                                               | `<FieldGroup>` wraps a **single** `<Field>`. FieldGroup exists to stack multiple fields at `gap-5`; with one child it contributes no spacing and is structural noise.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Acceptable as a convention anchor (keeps all forms shaped the same, ready for a 2nd field). Note only â€” keep for consistency, but be aware its `gap-5` is inert with one field.                                                                                                                          |
| `waitlist/page.tsx:102-166`                                                                             | Does **not** use `FieldGroup` and does not follow the stacked layout. It is an **inline CTA**: `<form className="flex w-full flex-col gap-2 sm:flex-row sm:items-center">` with `<Field className="relative w-full sm:flex-1">`, `sr-only` label, and an **absolutely-positioned** `FieldError` (`mt-1 ... sm:absolute sm:top-full`). Intentional (marketing one-field row), but it is a 2nd form layout with its own spacing (`gap-2`, not `gap-5`). | Keep as the documented **inline-CTA variant** (distinct from the stacked dialog-form convention). Don't force FieldGroup; do record it as a sanctioned second pattern so it isn't "fixed" into the stacked form later.                                                                                   |
| `waitlist/page.tsx:141` (Input `h-12 px-4 text-base`) + `:157` (Button `size="lg" h-12 px-6 text-base`) | Input height is overridden from the shadcn default `h-8` to `h-12` and re-sized to `text-base`; access/org-switcher use the bare `h-8` Input. So control height is **not** consistent across the three forms (`h-8` dialog vs `h-12` marketing).                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Acceptable as the marketing-CTA size tier, but it should be a named tier (input `h-12`/`text-base` + button `size="lg"`/`h-12`) rather than ad-hoc per-form overrides. Codify in the form conventions below.                                                                                             |
| `waitlist/page.tsx:161` vs `access.tsx:145` / `org-switcher.tsx:205`                                    | Spinner inconsistency: waitlist's pending spinner is `<RiLoaderLine className="animate-spin" />` (**no `size-5`**, no leading text â€” it _replaces_ the label), while the dialog forms render `size-5` spinner **leading** the persistent label. Same component, three slightly different renders.                                                                                                                                                     | Pick one spinner convention. Recommend: spinner **replaces** label text when the button is the sole action (waitlist), spinner **leads** retained label otherwise (dialogs) â€” but always size it (`size-4`/`size-5`) so it doesn't inherit `size-6` default. waitlist's unsized spinner is the lone gap. |
| `access.tsx:138` / `org-switcher.tsx:199`                                                               | Submit `<Button>` uses `variant="secondary"`; waitlist (`:154`) uses default (primary).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Different surfaces (dialog secondary action vs marketing primary CTA) â€” acceptable, but note the dialog-form submit is `secondary` by convention.                                                                                                                                                        |

**Pending-state source differs (not a bug, worth noting):** access drives disabled/spinner off a local `useState` `loader` flag (`loader === "email"`), org-switcher off a local `isOrgTransitioning` flag, waitlist off `mutation.isPending`. access/org-switcher hand-roll the pending flag instead of reading `form.state.isSubmitting` (org-switcher reads `form.state.isSubmitting` for the button but keeps the extra `isOrgTransitioning` for the broader org swap). Consider standardizing on `form.state.isSubmitting` / mutation `isPending` and dropping the redundant local flags where they only mirror submit state.

### Dialogs / overlays

**Default content (bare `<DialogContent>`, `dialog.tsx:53`):** `w-full max-w-[calc(100%-2rem)]` then `sm:max-w-sm` (24rem at â‰Ą640px), centered, `gap-4`, **`p-4`**, `rounded-xl`, `ring-1 ring-foreground/10`, auto close-button top-2/right-2. `DialogFooter` (`dialog.tsx:91`) is a full-bleed bar (`-mx-4 -mb-4 ... border-t bg-muted/50 p-4`). `DialogHeader` = `flex flex-col gap-2`.

**Sheet default (`sheet.tsx:56`):** `w-3/4` with `sm:max-w-sm`; popup has **no padding** (`SheetHeader`/`SheetFooter` carry their own `p-4`); `side` defaults to `"right"`.

Three non-`components/ui` overlay instances (full inventory, grep-confirmed): `access.tsx:90`, `org-switcher.tsx:160`, `navbar/home.tsx:167`. None hand-rolls the popup structure â€” all use the primitives.

| file:line              | issue                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | fix                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `access.tsx:90`        | `<DialogContent className="max-w-md sm:max-w-md">` â€” the `sm:max-w-md` is **redundant** (identical value to the base `max-w-md`, so the responsive variant changes nothing). It also overrides the default `sm:max-w-sm` to widen the sign-in dialog to `max-w-md` (28rem). | Drop the redundant `sm:max-w-md` â†’ just `className="max-w-md"`. The widening to `md` is a legitimate intent (auth dialog wants more room); keep it but express it once.                                                                                      |
| `org-switcher.tsx:160` | Bare `<DialogContent>` â†’ inherits the `sm:max-w-sm` (24rem) default. So the two app dialogs render at **different widths** (access `md` / 28rem vs org-switcher `sm` / 24rem) with no documented tiering.                                                                   | Decide intentionally: if both are "simple single-field" dialogs they should match. Recommend org-switcher stays default (`sm`) and access is the documented "auth/wider" tier (`md`) â€” see width tiers below. Just make the choice explicit, not incidental. |
| `navbar/home.tsx:167`  | `<SheetContent side="right">` â€” `side="right"` is already the `SheetContent` **default** (`sheet.tsx:42`), so the prop is redundant (harmless, but noise). Structure itself is correct: `SheetHeader`/`SheetTitle` used properly.                                           | Optional: drop `side="right"` to rely on the default, or keep it as explicit-intent documentation. Low priority; not a divergence in rendered output.                                                                                                        |

**Structure usage:** all three use the header primitives correctly. access wraps `DialogHeader`/`DialogTitle` in `sr-only` (visually-hidden title for a11y, real heading rendered in body) â€” fine. org-switcher uses `DialogHeader`+`DialogTitle`+`DialogDescription` as intended. **No `DialogFooter` is used in any instance** â€” both dialog forms put the submit button inline in the `<form>` rather than in a `DialogFooter` bar. That is consistent between the two (so not drift), but it means the `bg-muted/50` footer bar primitive is unused; the in-form full-width button is the de-facto dialog-form submit pattern. No overlay hand-rolls its own header/footer markup.

### Proposed conventions

**Standard form (stacked â€” dialogs & single-column forms):**

- Stack: native `<form>` â†’ `<FieldGroup>` (owns the `gap-5` rhythm) â†’ one `<Field>` per input â†’ `<FieldLabel htmlFor>` + `<Input>` + conditional `<FieldError errors=â€¦>`. **Put the submit `<Button>` inside `FieldGroup`** so a single gap governs the whole stack; remove the wrapper `space-y-4`.
- Validation: `@tanstack/react-form` + zod via `validators`; `isInvalid = isTouched && !isValid` â†’ `Field data-invalid` + `Input aria-invalid`.
- Submit: `w-full`, `disabled` on pending, **sized** leading spinner (`size-5`) before retained label. Use `form.state.isSubmitting` (or mutation `isPending`) as the single pending source; drop redundant local flags.
- Input: shadcn default (`h-8`, `text-sm`); no per-form height override.

**Standard form (inline CTA â€” marketing, e.g. waitlist):** sanctioned second variant. `<form className="flex flex-col gap-2 sm:flex-row sm:items-center">`, `sr-only` label, `<Field className="relative w-full sm:flex-1">` with absolutely-positioned `FieldError` (so the row height never shifts), `Input h-12 text-base`, submit `size="lg" h-12`. Spinner **replaces** the label. Document this as the "large inline CTA" tier so it isn't accidentally normalized into the stacked form.

**Standard dialog:**

- Width tiers (drive everything off the `dialog.tsx` `sm:max-w-sm` default): **`sm` (default, 24rem)** for simple single-field/confirm dialogs (org-switcher) Â· **`md` (28rem)** for auth / denser dialogs (access) Â· go wider only with intent. Always express the override **once** â€” never `max-w-md sm:max-w-md`.
- Padding/radius: inherit the primitive (`p-4`, `gap-4`, `rounded-xl`). Don't re-declare.
- Required structure: `DialogHeader` + `DialogTitle` (+ `DialogDescription` when there's body copy); `sr-only` the header only when a visible heading is rendered in the body. For dialog **forms**, the established pattern is an in-`<form>` full-width submit button (no `DialogFooter`); reserve `DialogFooter` for multi-action confirm dialogs.
- Sheets: rely on the `side="right"` default; use `SheetHeader`/`SheetTitle` (as `navbar/home.tsx` does).

## Interaction & feedback states

**Conventions:** loader = inline `RiLoaderLine animate-spin` in a disabled button; errors via the `unwrap` `{data,error}` envelope â†’ `throw` in queryFn â†’ `toast.error`; pending guards on async controls.

**Findings:**

- `components/ui/spinner.tsx` is dead code (defined, imported nowhere); every loader hand-rolls `RiLoaderLine` â€” source of the `size-4`/`size-5` drift and the missing `role="status"`/`aria-label`.
- `access.tsx:39-47` â€” `auth-providers` query has no error handling; an API failure makes all providers `false` and shows "No sign-in options configured" (error masquerades as empty config). **Real bug.**
- `components/ui/empty.tsx` exported, imported nowhere; hand-rolled empties at `access.tsx:224`, `org-switcher.tsx:130`.
- `user-menu.tsx:74-81` sign-out: no pending guard, no error handling. `org-switcher.tsx:138-150` menu items: no guard during transition.
- Toast copy drift: "Provider Not Found" (title case) vs sentence case; success "!" vs no end punctuation.
- Spinner-vs-label behavior differs (swap icon / replace label / append).

## Components, icons, accessibility

**Conventions:** uniform `@remixicon/react` (no stray libs, no duplicate concept-icons); `size-4` workhorse; Button-as-link via `render={<Link/>}`; color tokens.

**Findings:**

- `cursor-pointer` hand-duplicated ~19x across 10 files, with misses (home CTAs `page.tsx:250-268/670/763`, waitlist submit `:154`). **Centralize in `buttonVariants`.**
- In-button icon size split `size-4` vs `size-5` for the same role; `[&_svg]:size-4!` escape hatch in `mode-toggle.tsx:28`, `navbar/home.tsx:158`.
- `app/hire/page.tsx` â€” six `<h1>`s (sections `:248,277,292,315,332`); resume does it right. **a11y/SEO defect.**
- home/hire/resume have no `<main>` landmark (root `app/layout.tsx:66` drops children in `<body>`; only waitlist wraps `<main>`). **a11y.**
- `Empty`/`Item` exported, unused; `Badge` used once â€” hand-rolled pills (`api-status.tsx` x3, home hero `page.tsx:231`).
- `devtools.tsx:48` clickable icon: no button/aria; redundant `role="link"` on Button-as-anchor.
- Note: `DashboardHeader` correctly hand-rolls (page header owning `<h1>`, not an `Item` row).

## Data, navigation, theming

**Conventions:** typed `apiClient` + `unwrap` `{data,error}` everywhere; 100% `next/link` internal nav; no hardcoded URLs; tokenized theming via `next-themes`; `cn()` consistent.

**Findings:**

- `access.tsx:219-220` â€” dead `<a href="#">` Terms & Privacy links shipped. **Fix or remove.**
- Active-state matcher reimplemented 3 ways (`navbar/home.tsx`, `sidebar/console.tsx`, `sidebar/docs/content.tsx`) with different trailing-slash/prefix handling â†’ extract one `isActive` helper.
- `(protected)/layout.tsx:27` `.catch(() => {})` â€” house-style violation (prefer try/catch).
- `waitlist/page.tsx:27-31` queryFn swallows errors (the one query deviating from the throw pattern).
- `app/page.tsx:228` hardcoded `#80808012` grid; no `--success` token (green literals in `api-status.tsx`, `waitlist:98`). OG hex unavoidable (Satori).
- 3 raw `fetch()` (server getSession/set-active, the llms.txt route) â€” all justified.

## Consolidated: clear fixes vs decisions

### Real defects (fix regardless of standardization)

1. `access.tsx` auth-providers query â€” add error handling (API failure currently locks users out silently).
2. `app/hire/page.tsx` â€” demote section `<h1>`s to `<h2>`.
3. Add `<main>` landmark in `app/layout.tsx`; drop waitlist's own `<main>`.
4. `access.tsx:219` â€” fix/remove the dead `#` Terms & Privacy links.
5. `user-menu` sign-out + org-switcher menu items â€” pending guards + sign-out error handling.

### Clear mechanical fixes (no design call)

- Centralize `cursor-pointer` in `buttonVariants` (removes ~19 copy-pastes + the misses).
- Adopt `Spinner` everywhere (fixes loader size drift + a11y).
- Snap off-ladder one-offs: `gap-7.5`â†’`gap-8`, `size-7.5`â†’`size-8`, `size-4.5`â†’`size-5`, `w-45`â†’`min-w-48`, `text-[0.6rem]`â†’`text-xs`, `mb-18`â†’`mb-16`.
- `access.tsx` `max-w-md sm:max-w-md`â†’`max-w-md`; drop `space-y-4` vs FieldGroup `gap-5` conflict.
- `.catch(()=>{})`â†’try/catch; align the waitlist queryFn to the throw pattern.
- Extract one `isActive(pathname, href, {exact})` helper.

### Decisions (design language)

1. Two marketing scales (hire/resume `py-36`/`space-y-16`/Caveat vs home `py-24`/`font-bold`) â€” intentional, or align?
2. Heading weight `font-bold` vs `font-semibold` for marketing.
3. Viewport idiom `min-h-screen`/`svh`/`dvh` â†’ standardize on `dvh`?
4. Dialog width tiers (`sm` default / `md` auth) â€” formalize?
5. Shared container-gutter token, or leave marketing on `px-5`?
6. Add a `--success` token (mirror `--destructive`)?
7. Adopt `Empty`/`Item`/`Badge` for the hand-rolled equivalents?
8. `#80808012` grid â†’ a `--border`/`--color-grid` token?
